User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile within the NEU Library Access System, storing their basic information and access role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. Corresponds to Firebase Authentication UID.

role.description: The access role of the user within the system. (enum: 'admin', 'user')

collegeOffice.description: The college or office the user is affiliated with. Can be null.

isBlocked.description: Indicates if the user's access to the system is blocked. Defaults to false.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

VisitLog.title: VisitLog
VisitLog.description: Records each visit to the library by a user, including the purpose and time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VisitLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the visit. (Relationship: User 1:N VisitLog)

email.description: The email of the user at the time of the visit. Denormalized from User for easier querying and historical accuracy.

reason.description: The purpose or reason for the user's visit to the library.

timestamp.description: Server timestamp when the visit was recorded.

entryDate.description: The date of the visit in YYYY-MM-DD format for simplified date-based querying.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The document ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes the 'role' field for database access control (DBAC) and 'isBlocked' status. Provides explicit path-based ownership for individual users.

Stores individual visit logs, nested under the owning user's profile. The 'userId' field within each document, along with the hierarchical path, provides denormalized authorization context, enabling authorization independence. This structure supports secure QAPs for listing user-specific visit logs. Includes denormalized 'email' for historical accuracy.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this visit log. Corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: visitLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and secure `list` operations (QAPs) by leveraging hierarchical paths and strategic denormalization. 

1.  **Users Collection (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection where the document ID is the Firebase Authentication `uid`. This inherently links the document to its owner, simplifying authorization for individual user profile access (`request.auth.uid == userId`). The `role` field within each user document enables DBAC (Database Access Control) without requiring custom claims or `get()` calls on unrelated documents to determine the requesting user's role. This structure provides homogeneous security for all user profile documents: self-read/write and admin-read/write based on the `role` field of the requesting user.

2.  **Visit Logs Subcollection (`/users/{userId}/visit_logs/{visitLogId}`):** Visit logs are modeled as a subcollection under the respective user's document. This design ensures **strong ownership** and **authorization independence** for each `VisitLog` document. Each `VisitLog` document also explicitly includes the `userId` field. 
    *   **Authorization Independence:** To authorize access to a specific `VisitLog`, security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` from the path (`/users/{userId}/...`) or from the `resource.data.userId` field. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the parent `User` document, as all necessary authorization context (owner's UID) is present within the `VisitLog` document itself or its immediate path. This supports atomic operations, such as creating a `VisitLog` without needing to read the parent `User` document first.
    *   **QAPs (Secure List Operations):** For regular users, listing their own visit logs is straightforward and secure. A query to `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/visit_logs` directly targets the user's personal logs, and rules can easily enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`. For administrators, a collection group query on `visit_logs` can be used. Authorization for this admin-level `list` would rely on checking the `role` field of the requesting admin's own user document (e.g., `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'admin'`), which is a `get()` on the *requesting user's* document, not the parent of the listed items, thus maintaining Authorization Independence from the listed `VisitLog` documents themselves. The `email` field in `VisitLog` is denormalized from the `User` entity to provide historical accuracy and simplify queries, adhering to the invariants principle.
